metaTitle: Electrical Condition Installation Report (ECIR) by 3rd Party in Australia
metaDescription: Reliable way to find electrical faults is to obtain an electrical installation condition report (EICR) and fix them as recommended.
h2: Electrical Installation Certificate/Domestic Electrical Installation Certificate:
Where Proactive Safety Meets Power Engineering
paragraphs: As we all know, electricity is invisible to the body, so many people often forget to close their eyes to understand the state of electrical equipment and/or devices in the room. Especially when the hidden database is used in a high place in the computer room or closet.
Worn wires and faulty electrical appliances are the main causes of fires in the workplace.
A reliable way to solve these known faults is to have a qualified electrician perform the so-called fixed cable test to obtain an electrical installation condition report (EICR) .
Even though there is no mandatory requirement to have the latest EICR, business owners and building owners are legally responsible for the well-being of employees and tenants.

This means that if one of them is injured by a dangerous electric shock and you cannot provide evidence that you have taken the necessary steps to create a safe environment, you will be held responsible.
EICR determines that everything is installed and working as expected. If a fault is found, the electrician will detail the required troubleshooting and improvement suggestions in the report.
Specifically, EICR will record the inspection results and electrical conditions at that time, and provide detailed information about the following:
As per the national health and safety regulations, you must make sure all your electrical installations are safe, and regularly checked every 5 years.
The safe and reliable operation of the newly installed electrical system and related equipment requires several key components:
Between July 1- 2002, and October 31, 2011, Australian coroners reported 321 deaths from electric shocks, which have been identified and closed in the NCIS database.
Carelabs’ third-party certification includes an independent assessment to prove that the specified requirements for the product, personnel, process, or control system have been met.
We not only ensure and evaluate compliance with previously defined standards but also provide official certification marks or declarations of compliance.

Carelabs also provides regular inspections and tests; we will review facilities previously completed by other contractors and determine whether the facility is in a satisfactory condition to continue operation.

After completing any electrical work, it will be delivered to the customer. Describe in detail the type of work performed; any deviations from the rules listed in BS 7671 for design, installation, and testing work, and when the next verification should be carried out.
E (L) R Regulation 49(1): carrying out unsafe and substandard electrical work

The technicians offered by Carelabs are well qualified and familiar with electrical installations and always adhere to all the standards and regulations mentioned in Standards Australia (SA). We provide an electrical installation condition report as a third party to ensure the safety of your electrical equipment and your employees.
Carelabs offer EICR report in all the major cities of Queensland, New South Wales, South Australia, Victoria, Tasmania, and Western Australia.
Contact Carelabs to know more about EICR services and get a quote.
From fault analysis to electrical safety compliance, we have the right solution for you at Carelabs.
